title: CEO Commentary on Loan Condition – As of May 31, 2026
content:
Total loans stood at $19,912,409 as of May 31, 2026, reflecting an overall decline of -11.5% from the prior year end. The portfolio is primarily concentrated in used vehicle loans ($6,746,204) and new vehicle loans ($5,613,213), which together represent approximately 62.1% of total lending activity. Delinquency levels decreased by -9.2%, indicating improved repayment performance across the book.

Portfolio Composition:
The largest segment is used vehicle loans at $6,746,204 (33.9%), followed closely by new vehicle loans at $5,613,213 (28.2%). These two categories combined account for 62.1% of the total loan portfolio.

The remaining segments include all other unsecured loans/lines of credit ($5,049,676 or 25.4%), followed by secured non-real estate loans with a first lien at $1,133,512 (5.7%) and all other secured non-real estate lines of credit at $1,369,804 (6.9%). The total loan balance decreased -11.5% year-over-year.

Delinquency & Credit Quality:
Total delinquent loans amounted to $2,078,594 as of May 31, 2026, representing a decrease of -9.2%. Within this category, the largest exposure is used vehicle loans at $1,133,512 (accounting for approximately 54.5% of total delinquencies), followed by new vehicle loans at $782,053 (~37.6%).

Delinquency concentration diverges from portfolio composition: while used and new vehicle loans together comprise over two-thirds of the loan book, they also represent nearly 92% of all delinquent balances — indicating that credit risk remains disproportionately concentrated within these segments despite their overall decline in volume. Management continues to monitor exposure closely through targeted underwriting standards and ongoing account-level oversight.
